_meta.note: Revenue demand model by segment — sourced growth paths for each segment feeding Daedalus's SOTP. Not a dialed cone; each number is argued from a named driver.

PD_power_discrete.name: Power and Discrete (P&D incl. SiC)
growth_drivers: 800V EV architecture adoption: SiC is mandatory for 800V inverters (silicon can't handle thermal/switching losses). 800V EV models projected to grow from ~6M units in 2025 to ~15M in 2028 (Yole). At 6-8 SiC MOSFETs per inverter at $8-12 ASP each, the TAM grows ~2x by 2028.

STM's Sanan JV: joint venture with Sanan Optoelectronics for Chinese SiC market; provides local-for-local supply to BYD/CATL EV customers that will not use Western-supply-chain-only chips. Expected to add $300-500M revenue by 2027.

200mm SiC wafer transition: STM converting Catania fab to 200mm wafers in 2025-2027. 200mm SiC yields ~30% more die per wafer vs. 150mm and lowers cost per die by ~20-25%. This restores STM's cost competitiveness vs. Onsemi and Chinese suppliers.
growth_constraints: STM SiC market share declining: 37% (2022) -> 29% (2024). Not expected to reverse quickly given Onsemi's captive wafer supply and Chinese domestic preference. Base assumes stabilization at 22-24% by 2028.
Tesla structural headwind: Tesla's 75% SiC content reduction (Next Gen platform) structurally reduces STM's largest historical volume. Not recoverable even if broader market grows.
SiC industry overcapacity: Yole (Dec 2025) says utilization 50-70% and recovery takes until 2027-2028. Pricing pressure persists through at least 2026.
sources: Yole Power SiC Dec 2025 — SiC market $10.3B by 2030, overcapacity status
Yole Aug 2025 — automotive semi market share rankings
PGC Consultancy — Tesla 75% SiC reduction analysis

EMP_embedded_processing.name: Embedded Processing (STM32 MCU + automotive MCU + custom processing)
growth_drivers: STM32 restocking: industrial customers finished destocking by Q3 2025. Q1 2026 EMP grew +31% YoY, the strongest segment recovery. MCU content per industrial machine growing with IoT and edge AI trends.

Automotive MCU content growth: zone ECU architecture in EVs adds 3-5 automotive MCU units per vehicle vs 1-2 in legacy architecture. STM32 automotive variants benefit from EV platform ramp at BYD, Stellantis, BMW.

AI at the edge: STM32 devices with neural processing are seeing design-in acceleration for industrial AI inference applications. The STM32 ecosystem (800+ variants, STM32CubeIDE) is sticky — ecosystem switching costs are high.
growth_constraints: Chinese MCU competition: GigaDevice and Geehy offer STM32-compatible pinout MCUs at 10-20% price discount in cost-sensitive segments. This limits STM32's pricing power in commodity industrial applications.
Inventory overhang legacy: the 2023-2025 MCU destocking cycle was severe and some distributors may still have excess inventory in lower-velocity SKUs.
sources: STM Q1 2026 — EMP +31.3% YoY, strongest segment
Digitimes Mar 2026 — auto chipmakers exiting inventory reset

AMS_analog_mems_sensors.name: Analog, MEMS and Sensors (AM&S)
growth_drivers: NXP MEMS acquisition ($950M, closed Jul 2025): adds ~$300M annual automotive MEMS revenue, strengthens STM's automotive sensor position to compete with Bosch Sensortec and TDK/InvenSense. Management guides 'substantially accretive' gross margins.

Apple MEMS relationship: STM has supplied iPhone MEMS since ~2009. Apple iPhone installed base growing (>1.3B active iPhones). New Apple LiDAR iPhone 18 win (reported by Morgan Stanley, 2025) would add incremental revenue. Apple was 12.3% of STM total revenue in FY2023.
Industrial MEMS: MEMS sensors for industrial IoT and robotics growing 10-12% annually. STM's inertial MEMS leadership (accelerometers, gyroscopes) serves this market.
growth_constraints: Apple concentration risk: Apple is still the largest single MEMS customer. Any loss of iPhone MEMS socket or significant volume reduction would be material. Apple has historically dual-sourced from Bosch Sensortec.
Personal electronics cyclicality: smartphone unit growth is low-single digits. AM&S revenue was -1.9% FY2025 and only +23% YoY in Q1 2026 (partly NXP contribution).
sources: STM Q1 2026 — AM&S +23.2% YoY (NXP MEMS contribution)
STM press release Jul 2025 — NXP MEMS acquisition $950M, ~$300M annual revenue

RFOC_rf_optical.name: RF and Optical Communications (RF&OC — satellite + data center optical)
growth_drivers: SpaceX Starlink sole-source: STM holds ~90% market share in LEO satellite chip supply to Starlink. Management targeting $3B+ cumulative satellite revenue 2026-2028. UBS projects $1.3B satellite revenue by 2028. This is the most contracted/visible revenue stream in the group.

AWS data center engagement: February 2026 multi-billion multi-year strategic agreement for high-bandwidth connectivity (silicon photonics), mixed-signal processing, MCUs for infrastructure management, and power ICs for hyperscale. STM targeting $1B in 2026 and $2B in 2027. This segment is the swing variable.
Amazon Kuiper: Secondary LEO constellation satellite opportunity. STM is positioned as a natural supplier given Starlink expertise.
AI data center interconnect: silicon photonics / optical engines for 800G and 1.6T AI server interconnect is a $5B+ TAM growing at 30%+ per year.
growth_constraints: Data center ramp is unproven: AWS agreement is announced but revenue delivery is contingent on qualification, ramp, and sustained demand. From near-zero to $2B in 2 years would be one of the fastest semiconductor segment ramps in history.
SpaceX in-sourcing risk: SpaceX has been known to in-source components over time. Any decision to develop custom satellite semiconductors in-house would reduce STM's Starlink dependence.
Competition in silicon photonics: Coherent, II-VI/Finisar, and hyperscaler in-house teams are STM's competitors in optical interconnect. STM is a late entrant.
sources: Via Satellite May 2026 — STM $3B+ cumulative satellite revenue target 2026-2028
STM newsroom June 2026 — data center target raised to $1B 2026, $2B 2027
Mizuho May 2026 note — satellite >$3B cumulative, AI server content >$230M/GW
UBS June 2026 note — satellite $1.3B by 2028, data center $2.9B by 2028

base.note: Sum of segment bases: PD $2,000 + EMP $4,100 + AMS $5,300 + RFOC $2,400 = $13,800M for 2026. STM street consensus for FY2026 is ~$13.3B — our base is slightly above consensus given the data center guidance raise is recent. 2027-2028 extrapolates segment growth.
base.reconciliation_to_street: Street FY2026 consensus $13.3B vs our base $13.8B — we sit 4% above consensus because we include the post-June-2 data center revision that most analyst models predate. This is not aggressive; UBS models $14.7B for 2026.

bull.note: All segments fire: PD restocking beats, data center hits $1B in 2026 and $2B+ in 2027, satellite hits $1.3B by 2027. Requires BofA-level execution.

bear.note: Auto/industrial recovery stalls H2 2026, data center ramps slowly, SiC weakness persists. Revenue stays below 2024's $13.3B through 2028.
